title: Weather routing and fuel optimisation for shipping
summary: Ocean current, wave and wind data let ships optimise routes for fuel saving and emissions reduction.
problem: Ships consume more fuel and emit more CO2 when fighting adverse currents and waves. Without accurate, forward-looking ocean and wave forecasts, route planning cannot fully exploit favourable conditions or avoid costly weather.
solution: CMEMS provides global and regional forecasts of ocean currents, waves, sea level and wind. Integrated into weather-routing systems, these enable ships to optimise routes and speed, cutting fuel consumption and emissions while improving schedule reliability.

copernicusRole.detail: CMEMS currents, waves and wind feed a weather-routing engine together with vessel performance and schedule constraints. Copernicus supplies the metocean layer, not the route itself.
